entry.title: Middle East Cyber Battle Field Broadens — Especially in UAE
entry.content:
As the war with Iran continues, breach attempts targeting the United Arab Emirates tripled in a few weeks — many targeting critical infrastructure.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Attacks Abuse Windows Phone Link to Steal Texts & Bypass 2FA
entry.content:
In hard-to-detect attacks, hackers are dropping the CloudZ RAT and a fresh plug-in, Pheno, to hijack the Windows-based bridge between PCs and smartphones.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: From Stuxnet to ChatGPT: 20 News Events That Shaped Cyber
entry.content:
As part of its 20th anniversary celebration, Dark Reading looks back on 20 of the biggest newsmaking events from the past two decades that influenced the risk landscape for today's cybersecurity teams.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Instructure Breach Exposes Schools' Vendor Dependence
entry.content:
ShinyHunters' attack on Instructure, which owns the widely used Canvas learning management system (LMS), carries big questions about the trust educational institutions put into their vendors.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Palo Alto Networks to Patch Zero-Day Exploited to Hack Firewalls
entry.content:
CVE-2026-0300 affects the Captive Portal service of PAN-OS software on PA and VM series firewalls. The post Palo Alto Networks to Patch Zero-Day Exploited to Hack Firewalls appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Oracle Debuts Monthly Critical Security Patch Updates
entry.content:
Containing fixes for critical-severity vulnerabilities, the monthly rollouts will focus on addressing priority issues faster. The post Oracle Debuts Monthly Critical Security Patch Updates appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Government, Scientific Entities Hit via Daemon Tools Supply Chain Attack
entry.content:
While trojanized Daemon Tools versions were installed worldwide, a sophisticated backdoor was dropped only on a dozen systems. The post Government, Scientific Entities Hit via Daemon Tools Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: CISA Launches ‘CI Fortify’ to Prepare Critical Infrastructure for Geopolitical Cyber Conflict
entry.content:
Agency issued guidance and calls on operators to build resilient OT environments capable of surviving extended isolation and cyber compromise. The post CISA Launches ‘CI Fortify’ to Prepare Critical Infrastructure for Geopolitical Cyber Conflict appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Romanian Man Extradited to US for Role in Hacking Scheme 17 Years Ago
entry.content:
Gavril Sandu, 53, was indicted in 2017, but was arrested and extradited to the United States only in 2026. The post Romanian Man Extradited to US for Role in Hacking Scheme 17 Years Ago appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Iranian APT Intrusion Masquerades as Chaos Ransomware Attack
entry.content:
Likely perpetrated by MuddyWater, the attack combined social engineering, persistence, credential harvesting, and data theft. The post Iranian APT Intrusion Masquerades as Chaos Ransomware Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Herd Security Raises $3 Million for AI-Powered Training Platform
entry.content:
The startup will invest in expanding its training categories, optimizing video generation, and growing its partnership ecosystem. The post Herd Security Raises $3 Million for AI-Powered Training Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Autonomous Offensive Security Firm XBOW Raises $35 Million
entry.content:
The company raised another $35 million as an extension to its previously announced Series C funding round. The post Autonomous Offensive Security Firm XBOW Raises $35 Million appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Claude AI Guided Hackers Toward OT Assets During Water Utility Intrusion
entry.content:
Dragos has published a report describing how threat actors used Claude AI in an attack on a water and drainage utility in Mexico. The post Claude AI Guided Hackers Toward OT Assets During Water Utility Intrusion appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: FTC bans data broker Kochava from selling sensitive location info
entry.content:
The FTC has said that Kochava sold precise geolocation data showing consumers visiting houses of worship and health care clinics without their consent or awareness, an alleged violation of a law barring companies from engaging in unfair and deceptive practices.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Hackers compromise Daemon Tools in global supply-chain attack, researchers say
entry.content:
Researchers at Kaspersky said attackers tampered with installers for Daemon Tools — a popular program used to mount disk images as virtual drives — and distributed them through the software’s official website.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: New CISA initiative aims for critical infrastructure to operate offline during cyberattacks
entry.content:
The initiative, named CI Fortify, focuses on isolation and recovery efforts that would see critical infrastructure organizations proactively disconnect from third-party dependencies and find ways to operate without reliable telecommunications and internet.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Palo Alto warns of critical software bug used in firewall attacks
entry.content:
A patch for the bug, tracked as CVE-2026-0300, has not been published yet and Palo Alto Networks said it will be included in releases over the next two weeks.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: North Korean hackers targeted ethnic Koreans in China with Android ‘BirdCall’ malware
entry.content:
Researchers at cybersecurity firm ESET attributed the campaign to APT37 and said the hackers used a backdoor attached to a suite of card games from a company called Sqgame.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
entry.content:
AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and n
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
entry.content:
A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
entry.content:
The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
entry.content:
A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
entry.content:
An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
entry.content:
Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
entry.content:
A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Frequency and Severity of Hacks of Medical Devices Increasing
entry.content:
Healthcare organizations are increasingly concerned about medical device security and for good reason – attacks targeting or impacting medical devices […] The post Frequency and Severity of Hacks of Medical Devices Increasing appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Free Webinar: HIPAA Email Security 101: PHI, Encryption, and What’s Required
entry.content:
According to the Paubox 2026 Healthcare Email Security Report, in 2025, 170 email-related data breaches were reported to the HHS’ […] The post Free Webinar: HIPAA Email Security 101: PHI, Encryption, and What’s Required appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Delta Dental Fined $2.25 Million Over 2023 MOVEit Transfer Hack
entry.content:
Delta Dental Insurance and Delta Dental of New York (Delta Dental) have agreed to pay a fine of $2.25 million […] The post Delta Dental Fined $2.25 Million Over 2023 MOVEit Transfer Hack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: CMS Found to Have Leaked Providers’ SSNs
entry.content:
A database created by the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) has been exposed online, exposing providers’ Social Security […] The post CMS Found to Have Leaked Providers’ SSNs appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Alpine Ear, Nose, & Throat Settles Class Action Data Breach Lawsuit
entry.content:
Alpine Ear, Nose, & Throat, a Fort Collins, Colorado-based healthcare provider with multiple locations in the state of Colorado, has […] The post Alpine Ear, Nose, & Throat Settles Class Action Data Breach Lawsuit appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: RXNT Notifies Customers About Cybersecurity Incident and Data Breach
entry.content:
Networking Technology, Inc., doing business as RXNT, a healthcare software technology company that provides electronic health record software, has started […] The post RXNT Notifies Customers About Cybersecurity Incident and Data Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Starr Insurance Discloses Ransomware Attack
entry.content:
The health insurance company Starr Insurance has disclosed a ransomware attack and data breach. Data breaches have also been reported […] The post Starr Insurance Discloses Ransomware Attack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: soavegel[.]it
entry.content:
Verification alert Listings attributed to SAFEPAY have been reported as including unverified or fabricated victim...
🎭 Threat Actor: SAFEPAY | 📰 Source: Red Packet Security | 🏷️ Type: Ransomware

Verification alert Listings attributed to M3RX have been reported as including unverified or fabricated victim...
🎭 Threat Actor: M3RX | 📰 Source: Red Packet Security | 🏷️ Type: Ransomware

entry.title: Vimeo data breach exposes personal information of 119,000 people
entry.content:
The ShinyHunters extortion gang stole personal information belonging to over 119,000 people after hacking the Vimeo online video platform in April, according to data breach notification service Have I Been Pwned. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Student hacked Taiwan high-speed rail to trigger emergency brakes
entry.content:
A 23-year-old university student in Taiwan was arrested for interfering with the TETRA communication system used by the country's high-speed railway network (THSR). [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: DAEMON Tools trojanized in supply-chain attack to deploy backdoor
entry.content:
Hackers trojanized installers for the DAEMON Tools software and since April 8, delivered a backdoor to thousands of systems that downloaded the product from the official website. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Instructure hacker claims data theft from 8,800 schools, universities
entry.content:
The hacker behind a breach at education technology giant Instructure claims to have stolen 280 million data records for students and staff from 8,809 colleges, school districts, and online education platforms. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Palo Alto Networks warns of firewall RCE zero-day exploited in attacks
entry.content:
Palo Alto Networks warned customers today that a critical-severity unpatched vulnerability in the PAN-OS User-ID Authentication Portal is being exploited in attacks. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: MuddyWater hackers use Chaos ransomware as a decoy in attacks
entry.content:
The MuddyWater Iranian hackers disguised their operations as a Chaos ransomware attack, relying on Microsoft Teams social engineering to gain access and establish persistence. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Why ransomware attacks succeed even when backups exist
entry.content:
Backups don't fail because they're missing, they fail because attackers destroy them first. Acronis explains how ransomware targets backup systems before encryption, leaving no path to recovery. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: DAEMON Tools devs confirm breach, release malware-free version
entry.content:
Disc Soft Limited, the maker of DAEMON Tools Lite, confirmed that the software had been trojanized in a supply chain attack and released a new, malware-free version. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Critical vm2 sandbox bug lets attackers execute code on hosts
entry.content:
A critical vulnerability in the popular Node.js sandboxing library vm2 allows escaping the sandbox and executing arbitrary code on the host system. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Hackers abuse Google ads for GoDaddy ManageWP login phishing
entry.content:
A phishing campaign delivered through Google sponsored search results is targeting credentials for ManageWP, GoDaddy's platform for managing fleets of WordPress websites. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Instructure discloses second data breach in less than a year
entry.content:
Instructure defines itself as the “O.G. champions of open edtech. The makers of Canvas, Mastery, and Parchment (solutions for learning, assessment, and credentialing). Host of the world’s largest online community of educators. (And yes—we’re ‘the panda people.’). We build industry-leading edtech, empowering both teachers and learners at every step of their journey.” Sadly, they were... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Summary of Court Approved Legal Notice for Pennsylvania State Education Association Data Incident Settlement
entry.content:
PHILADELPHIA, May 4, 2026 /PRNewswire/ — The following release was issued by RG/2 Claims Administration LLC, as Settlement Administrator. If Your Private Information Was Potentially Compromised in a Data Incident Announced by the Pennsylvania State Education Association on March 18, 2025, You May be Eligible for Benefits from a Class Action Settlement. A proposed Settlement has been... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Medicare portal database exposed health providers’ Social Security numbers
entry.content:
Dan Diamond and Clara Ence Morse report: The Trump administration inadvertently exposed the Social Security numbers of health care providers in a database powering a new Medicare portal, The Washington Post found. The Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) last year created a directory to help seniors look up which doctors and medical providers accept... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Latvian national involved with Karakurt and other ransomware gangs sentenced for his role in ransomware organization
entry.content:
From the DOJ’s press release: A Latvian national was sentenced today to 102 months in prison for his role in a major Russian ransomware organization that stole from and extorted over 54 companies. According to court documents, Deniss Zolotarjovs (Денисс Золотарёвс), 35, of Moscow, Russia, was a member of a ransomware organization led by former... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: NYC Public Schools Lack Central Inventory to Track Vendors Used By Schools — NYS Auditor
entry.content:
Audit conducted by NYS Comptroller’s Office between 2020-2025 found multiple concerns leaving students and employees at risk of privacy and data security breaches. The auditor also criticized the city for failing to cooperate in a timely manner with the auditor’s requests for information. In June 2014, a decade after the NYC Education Department had been... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Vimeo data breach exposes personal information of 119,000 people
entry.content:
Sergiu Gatlan reports: The ShinyHunters extortion gang stole personal information belonging to over 119,000 people after hacking the Vimeo online video platform in April, according to data breach notification service Have I Been Pwned. Vimeo is a video hosting and streaming platform publicly traded on the Nasdaq stock market, with over 300 million registered users... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: DeFi Investors Pull $14 Billion Following Cyberattacks
entry.content:
PYMNTS reports: Cryptocurrency investors are reportedly exiting the decentralized finance (DeFi) space following two high-profile hacks. Close to $14 billion has been pulled from DeFi projects in recent weeks, the Financial Times (FT) reported Wednesday (May 6), citing the data firm DefiLlama. This came after hackers tied to the North Korean government stole $290 million from the KelpDAO platform and used... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: When Your Vendor’s Breach Becomes Your Lawsuit: Privacy Risk Lessons from Recent Bank Litigation
entry.content:
Nancy Eff Presnell, Gene F. Price, and Matthew R. Schantz write: A recent high-profile incident illustrates the growing litigation and regulatory risks that financial institutions face from vendor-driven data breaches. Within weeks of a national bank confirming a data security incident at a third-party service provider, at least two putative class actions were filed, though... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Missouri regulators escalate pressure on Conduent over data breach potentially affecting millions
entry.content:
Sarah Motter reports: Missouri regulators say a major national vendor is stonewalling their investigation into a cybersecurity breach that could affect millions of consumers. The Missouri Department of Commerce and Insurance now says it is escalating its response to the cybersecurity breach at Conduent Business Services. Conduent is a national vendor that handles sensitive insurance... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: ‘GothFerrari’ Sentenced to 78 Months in Prison for Role in Massive Cryptocurrency Heist
entry.content:
He’s 20 years old and has just been sentenced to 6 1/2 years in prison. The U.S. Attorney’s Office for the District of Columbia announced today: WASHINGTON – Marlon Ferro, 20, of Santa Ana, California, was sentenced today in U.S. District Court to 78 months in prison in connection with his role in a sprawling social engineering conspiracy that stole well over $250 million... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Unknown
entry.content:
Unknown reported a healthcare breach.
📰 Source: HHS OCR | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Marin Cancer Care
entry.content:
Marin Cancer Care reported a data breach in California.
